title: Spin-polarized Josephson current induced by inhomogeneous altermagnetic interlayers
authors: Wenjun Zhao, Yuri Fukaya, Pablo Burset, Jorge Cayao, Yukio Tanaka, Bo Lu
abstract: The pursuit of dissipationless spin supercurrents is a central theme in superconducting spintronics. We propose a field-free Josephson junction using an inhomogeneous altermagnetic interlayer with in-plane Néel vectors. We show that the current-phase relation and critical Josephson current is highly sensitive to the misorientation angle between the altermagnetic layers' Néel vectors. Specifically, at a $π$ misorientation with equal layer thicknesses the spatial oscillations of the superconducting pair amplitude, governed by the center-of-mass momentum, undergo mutual cancellation. This compensation suppresses individual layer pair-breaking, significantly enhancing the critical current and eliminating $0$-$π$ transitions. Furthermore, the non-collinear alignment of the Néel vectors facilitates the emergence of a net spin-polarized Josephson current. This spin current serves as a distinct signature of spin-triplet pair correlations, generated by the spin-dependent momentum shifts inherent to the altermagnetic exchange field. Our results establish a highly tunable, field-free platform for the realization of dissipationless spintronic devices.
summary: This paper proposes a way to create a field-free platform for superconducting spintronics using altermagnetic layers. By tuning the orientation of the altermagnetic Neel vectors, one can control the magnitude and polarity of spin-polarized supercurrents and eliminate unwanted 0-pi transitions. This provides a highly tunable mechanism for generating dissipationless spin currents.
main_problem: The study aims to explore how inhomogeneous altermagnetic interlayers can be used to tailor superconducting spin currents and manipulate 0-pi transitions in Josephson junctions without the need for external magnetic fields.
main_result: The researchers found that a pi-misorientation of Neel vectors in altermagnetic layers suppresses pair-breaking and eliminates 0-pi transitions, while non-collinear alignment generates a tunable, spin-polarized Josephson current.
method: The study employs a tight-binding model on a 2D lattice and uses the recursive Green's function method combined with the Mobius transformation to calculate the nonlocal part of the Matsubara Green's function.
model_or_system: A 2D Josephson junction consisting of two superconducting leads connected by two altermagnetic layers and a normal region (SC1-AM1-N-AM2-SC2), featuring anisotropic spin splitting with d-wave symmetry.
key_observables: Josephson charge current (Ic), spin-polarized Josephson current (Is), and the current-phase relation (CPR).
important_parameters: Altermagnetic strength (J), misorientation angle between Neel vectors (delta chi), junction length (L), and interface transparency (tint).
main_assumptions: The system assumes translational invariance along the y-direction, a small number of sites for the normal region, and the use of a conventional BCS pairing potential.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a schematic of the junction setup, illustrating the spin-band lobes and Neel vectors; Figure 4(d) shows the complex oscillatory behavior of the maximum spin supercurrent as a function of crystal misorientation.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of field-free spintronics, describes the altermagnetic Josephson junction model and Hamiltonian, details the Green's function numerical method, presents results regarding charge and spin currents under different Neel vector orientations, and concludes with experimental implications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Sketch of a two-dimensional SC1–AM1–N–AM2–SC2 Josephson junction on the tight-binding lattice. The black and red ellipses represent the two spin species of the al- termagnetic spin bands in momentum space and ˆn1(2) = (cos χ1(2), sin χ1(2), 0) denotes the in-plane N´eel vector in AM1 (AM2), i.e., the tilt angle of the spin-band lobe relative to the x axis. Misorientation of spin triplet pair amplitude with non- collinear AMs drives an out-of-plane spin supercurrent Is.

FIG. 2. Andreev bound state spectra at (a)(d) ky = 0 and (b)(e) ky = 0.1π. (c)(f) Total Josephson current. (a)-(c) correspond to relative orientations χ1 = 0 and χ2 = 0; (d)-(f) correspond to χ1 = 0 and χ2 = π. Blue solid and red dashed lines denote junction lengths L = 22a and L = 38a, respectively. J = 0.25t for all panels. I0 represents the maximum Josephson current of a two-dimensional SC-N-SC junction with a two-site normal interlayer.

FIG. 3. Parameter dependence of the critical charge current Imax c . (a) Imax c as a function of junction length L (from 2a to 70a) for J = 0.25t. (b) Imax c as a function of J for L = 32a. Different colors denote various χ2 with χ1 = 0 fixed.

FIG. 4. (a) Spin supercurrent Is as a function of the super- conducting phase difference φ with a fixed crystal misorien- tation χ2 −χ1 = π/2. (b) Spin supercurrent Is at zero phase difference (φ = 0) as a function of the relative orientation χ2 −χ1. (c) Maximum spin supercurrent Imax s versus the junction length L for χ2 −χ1 = π/2. (d) Maximum spin su- percurrent Imax s as a function of the misorientation χ2 −χ1. In all panels, J = 0.25t, the black curves correspond to the dx2−y2 case with χ1 = 0 and the red curves to the dxy case with χ1 = π/4. For panels (a), (b), and (d), the junction length is fixed at L = 22a.
